text: For languages A and B, let the perfect shuffle of A and B be the language {w | w = a1b1...akbk, where a1...ak in A and b1...bk in B, each ai,bi in Sigma}. Show that the class of regular languages is closed under perfect shuffle.
Prove using pumping lemma, the following language is not regular L={w in {0,1}* | w contains more 0's than 1's}.

Design a CFG for the language L={ww^R zz^R | w,z in {0,1}+, 011 is a substring of w and |z| is odd, w^R is the reverse of w, z^R is the reverse of z}. Explain the use of each production in the constructed grammar.
Convert the given Context free grammar G into an equivalent context free grammar G1 in Chomsky normal form (CNF): S -> aXbX, X -> aY | bX | lambda, Y -> X | c.
Show that the language L = {beta#beta^R#beta | where Sigma = {a,c,#} and beta in {a,c}*} is not context free (beta^R is the reverse of beta).
Write the above language L (in Question 3(a)) as the intersection of two context-free languages (over Sigma).

Consider the following context free grammar (where A is the start symbol): A -> BA | AC | a, B -> CB | BA | b, C -> AC | CB | a. Apply CYK algorithm for the input string abbaa. Find the nonterminal set where X is marked in the table. Without steps full marks will not be awarded.

Give a push down automata (PDA) that recognizes the language L of all strings w in {0,1}* such that the first and last symbol of w are the same, and moreover, if the length of w is odd, then the middle symbol of w is different from the first and the last. For example, strings 00, 11, 010, 010100010010, 0101010 are in L, but lambda, 0, 1, 000, 111, 110, 00001, 1001001 are not. Show the configurations made by the constructed PDA for the input strings (a) 0101010 (b) 111.

The derivative of a language for a string x is defined as follows: L_x = { y | xy in L }. 1) What is L_aa for the language L={a^(n+1)b^n | n >= 1}. (1 mark) 2) What is L_b for the language L={a^(n+1)b^n | n >= 1}. (1 mark) 3) Prove that when L is regular, the derivative of L (ie., L_x) is also regular. (3 marks)
Consider the language L1 = {a^p b^q a^r | pq, r >= 1 and p+q != r}. Prove or disprove that the language L is regular.
Design a CFG for the language Upsilon = {a^n b^m a^(2n) : n, m >= 0}.

Consider the grammar S -> 0S1 | 1S0 | lambda, where S is the start symbol, {0,1} are terminals. You can see that for every 0 there is a 1 and vice versa in the rule (other than S -> lambda). Is the grammar generates the language L2 = {w : |w|_0 = |w|_1} where |w|_a refers to the occurrences of a in w. If not, justify with a string in the language L2 that cannot be produced by the given grammar rules. What further changes you need to do in the given grammar in order to generate L2.
Construct the CYK algorithm for the following CFG and check whether the string "aabcb" is derivable from the grammar. The Start Symbol is A. A -> CB, B -> DC | b, C -> BD | a, D -> AD | c.
Construct the given Context Free Grammar G=(V,T,P,S) into Chomsky normal form. P is the start symbol. P -> RPT, Q -> RP/Ra, R -> bRPR|b|epsilon, T -> PaP|R|aa.
Derive the CFG for the given Context Free Language L = {v^r i^(r+s) t^s | r, s >= 0} and check the ambiguity of the given grammar.

The language L have a strings of balanced "#" symbol and "@" symbol: every "#" symbol can be paired with a unique subsequent "@" symbol, and every "@" symbol can be paired with a unique preceding "#" symbol. Moreover, the string between any such pair has the same property. Derive the language for the given condition and Construct the PDA for the derived language.

Construct the pushdown automata that recognize the language L = {a^(2m) c^(4n) d^n d^m, where n, m >= 0}. Note: Push one input alphabet at a time; similarly, pop one alphabet from top of the stack at a time.
Convert the given DFA into Regular Expression using Arden's (or) State Elimination Method. Note: Here the q_d and q_f are the final states.

Evaluate the following grammars G1 and G2 may or may not be ambiguous. If it is ambiguous, indicate the grammar by constructing two or more parse trees with the shortest string. G1: S -> AB | aaB, A -> aA | epsilon, B -> bB | b. G2: S -> aAb | abB, A -> ab, B -> epsilon.

Evaluate the following grammars G3 and G4 may or may not be finite or infinite. Let G be the grammar defined by (V,T,P,S). Also check if the grammar is regular or CFG and justify it with appropriate reasons. Also present the intermediate results in Table I. G3: S -> AB | aaB, A -> aA | epsilon, B -> bB | epsilon. G4: S -> A | B, A -> aAb | ab, B -> abB | epsilon.

Construct an equivalent CNF grammar G from the following CFG. Let G5 be the grammar defined by (V,T,P,S). Also specify the intermediate results in Table II. G5: S -> AS | ABA | CD, A -> aB, B -> Bb | epsilon, C -> aCb | Cb, D -> aBa | d, E -> DE | e.

Consider the following transition table for a Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA) with states q0, q1, q2. Identify the language accepted by this DFA and provide at least two example strings that belong to this language. Using Arden's Theorem, derive a regular expression that represents the language recognized by this automaton. Clearly show the system of equations formed and justify each step in your derivation.

Grammars can be classified based on their structural properties, one of which is linearity. A Linear Grammar is a Context-Free Grammar (CFG) in which there is at most one non-terminal on the right-hand side of each production. Understanding the difference between Left-Linear Grammars (LLG) and Right-Linear Grammars (RRG) is essential for converting a given grammar into its equivalent form. Consider the following Left-Linear Grammar (LLG) and convert it into Right-Linear Grammars (RRG): S -> Aa | Ba | a, A -> Bb | Cb, B -> Cc | b, C -> Sd | c.

Consider the grammar with the following productions: S -> aS | SbS | lambda. I. Discuss the ambiguity of the grammar and determine if the given grammar is ambiguous or not. (5 Marks) II. Find the derivation trees for the string "aababaa" and identify two more strings in L(G) with derivation trees of height five or greater. (5 Marks)

The following grammar contains redundant or unnecessary productions: S -> AB | aB, A -> aA | B | a, B -> bB | C | lambda, C -> cC | c. Identify and eliminate any useless symbols in the given grammar. Then, simplify the grammar while ensuring that it still generates the same language. After simplification, convert the resulting grammar into Greibach Normal Form (GNF).

Construct a PDA that recognizes the following language: L = {a^n b^m a^m b^n | n, m >= 1}. Clearly define the transitions and explain how the PDA processes input strings. Provide a brief explanation of how the PDA ensures the correct acceptance condition with example.

(a) Convert the following Grammar into Finite automata: S -> aA | bB, A -> aA | bS | c, B -> bB | a. (3 Marks) (b) Let R1 and R2 be regular languages over Sigma. Then, exclusive of R1 and R2 is defined as EXC(R1,R2) = { x in Sigma* : x in {R1-R2} or x in {R2-R1}}. Prove that EXC(R1,R2) is regular. (3 Marks) (c) Using pumping lemma, demonstrate that the given language L = {x^i y^j z^k | k <= i+j} is not regular. (4 Marks)

Consider the following context free grammar (where S is the start symbol): S -> AB | BC, A -> BA | a, B -> CC | b, C -> AB | c. Apply CYK algorithm with all required steps for the input string "abbca".

Consider the following grammar (where U is the start symbol) remove all unit-productions, all useless productions, and all lambda-productions from the grammar where Sigma = {0,1}: U -> 0V | 0WW, V -> 0V | lambda, W -> 1W | 11X, X -> W. After simplification convert the grammar into an equivalent grammar in Chomsky normal form. (7 Marks)
Show that the following grammar is ambiguous. What happens for the following string aaaab. Discuss in terms of parse tree. S -> AB | aaaB, A -> a | Aa, B -> b. (3 Marks)
Convert the following grammar to an equivalent grammar in GNF: S -> AB, A -> BB | a, B -> AB | a. (6 Marks)
Consider the context-free languages L1 = {a^(2n) b^k c^n | n,k >= 1} and L2 = {a^m b^(2p) c^p | m,p >= 1}. What is L1 intersect L2? Is it a context-free language? Justify briefly. (4 Marks)

Construct a Non-deterministic push down automata (NPDA) that recognizes the language L = {ab(ab)^n x b(ab)^n a | n >= 0, x in {a,b}}. Note that x is a symbol a or b. Describe the idea of your construction of transition rules and provide the transition rules. Show the configurations made by the constructed NPDA for the input string "ababababa".
